params.name: Abel Prize
params.description:
March 23, 2003 AD, marks the first presentation of the Abel Prize in Oslo, Norway.
Abel was a young and talented mathematician who died in his prime, and in order to commemorate his great contributions, Norway established the Abel Prize, a mathematical award. Compared to the highest award in mathematics before, the Fields Medal, the Abel Prize has an award of eight hundred thousand US dollars, several hundred times that of the Fields Medal, playing a considerably positive role in raising the …
